deliveries.name: Queryable ADOS project catalog
deliveries.description: Lossless extraction + classification of the ChatGPT export into a private, queryable catalog.
deliveries.materiality_rationale: Acceptance depends on lossless capture and queryability.

process_evidence_materiality.rationale: process/evidence supports a delivery but is not independently accepted here

deliveries.acceptance_context: Accepted when extraction is lossless and the catalog is queryable.

deliveries.name: Model/provider benchmark instrument
deliveries.description: Same-workload evaluation reporting completion, depth-on-success, accuracy, schema-validity, speed, energy, cost as separate axes.
deliveries.materiality_rationale: This delivery realizes the Goal: a trustworthy decision instrument.

process_evidence_materiality.rationale: process/evidence supports a delivery but is not independently accepted here

deliveries.acceptance_context: Accepted when each axis is reported separately, failures excluded from depth, accuracy adjudicated vs a reference.

reference_class.applicability_notes: structured-extraction workload on real personal data

deliveries.name: Keep-vs-return / local-vs-cloud verdict
deliveries.description: The reproducible economic decision derived from the benchmark instrument.
deliveries.materiality_rationale: The Goal's purpose: a grounded build-vs-buy decision.

process_evidence_materiality.rationale: process/evidence supports a delivery but is not independently accepted here

deliveries.acceptance_context: Accepted when the verdict is reproducible and derived only from separated coordinates.

project_coordinates.name: Provider completion / reliability
project_coordinates.definition: Fraction of the shared bundle workload a provider returns a schema-valid result for, on the same items every other provider sees.
project_coordinates.measures: Reliability: did the provider finish the work item at all.
does_not_measure: Whether the returned content is deep, or correct. A blank-but-returned item still counts as a failure if not schema-valid.

best_in_class_basis.description: codex/cloud providers reach ~100% completion on this workload; that is the reference ceiling.
best_in_class_basis.applicability_notes: structured-extraction workload, single-user WSL home lab

measurement_contract.quality_question: What fraction of attempted items returned a schema-valid result?
measurement_contract.measurement_method: llm_ok==true count / attempted count, per run-label, over the identical bundle set (FR-B1).

anchors.delivery_state: no item returns schema-valid output
only a minority of items complete; the provider fails most of the workload
about half the workload completes
majority complete but a material minority fail
nearly all items complete; only isolated failures remain
every attempted item returns schema-valid output

measurement_contract.target_definition: 100 == every item in the shared workload returns schema-valid output for this provider, this run.

interaction_refs.rationale: completion must not be bought by slowing throughput

project_coordinates.name: Depth-on-success (field fill over completed items only)
project_coordinates.definition: Mean ADOS-record fill (goal/objectives/requirements/archetype-fields) computed over COMPLETED items only — never over the full workload.
project_coordinates.measures: How richly a provider fills the ADOS record WHEN it succeeds.
does_not_measure: Whether the filled content is correct (a model can fill three wrong objectives and score full depth), and whether the provider is reliable.

best_in_class_basis.description: codex depth ~99% on completed items is the practical reference.
best_in_class_basis.applicability_notes: structured-extraction workload, single-user WSL home lab

measurement_contract.quality_question: Over completed items only, how fully is the ADOS record populated?
measurement_contract.measurement_method: mean(goal_present, min(objectives,3)/3, min(requirements,3)/3, archetype_fields_fill) over items where llm_ok==true.
evidence_requirements: explicit exclusion of llm_ok:false from the denominator

anchors.delivery_state: completed items are empty shells
partial fill on completed items
completed items are fully populated
required_conditions: all four fill axes saturated on completed items
exclusion_conditions: any failed item included in the mean

measurement_contract.target_definition: 100 == completed items saturate all four fill axes. Failed items are excluded, not scored zero.

interaction_refs.rationale: richer fill can mask wrong classification

project_coordinates.name: Classification accuracy vs reference
project_coordinates.definition: Agreement of primary archetype and primary domain against a designated reference answer key (codex), over comparable completed items.
project_coordinates.measures: Correctness of the classification a provider produced.
does_not_measure: Depth of fill or reliability. A clean, fully-filled record can still be wrong (qwen3:8b: 85% depth, 16% accuracy).

best_in_class_basis.description: codex is the reference; agreement is measured against it over the comparable set.
best_in_class_basis.applicability_notes: structured-extraction workload, single-user WSL home lab

measurement_contract.quality_question: Does the provider's archetype/domain match the reference key?
measurement_contract.measurement_method: primary_archetype agreement and primary_domain agreement vs ref over comparable completed items; report both, headline the lower.

anchors.delivery_state: classifications systematically disagree with the reference
required_evidence: per-slug agreement table vs the reference run-label

anchors.delivery_state: agrees only by chance on the easiest items
required_conditions: agreement ≈ 10% on the lower of archetype/domain over comparable completed items
required_evidence: per-slug agreement table vs the reference run-label

anchors.delivery_state: agrees on a small, easy subset; misses most archetypes/domains
required_conditions: agreement ≈ 20% on the lower of archetype/domain over comparable completed items
required_evidence: per-slug agreement table vs the reference run-label

anchors.delivery_state: agrees on the obvious cases, wrong on anything specific
required_conditions: agreement ≈ 30% on the lower of archetype/domain over comparable completed items
required_evidence: per-slug agreement table vs the reference run-label

anchors.delivery_state: agrees on common types, systematically wrong on specific ones
required_conditions: agreement ≈ 40% on the lower of archetype/domain over comparable completed items
required_evidence: per-slug agreement table vs the reference run-label

anchors.delivery_state: even split — half the classifications match the reference
required_conditions: agreement ≈ 50% on the lower of archetype/domain over comparable completed items
required_evidence: per-slug agreement table vs the reference run-label

anchors.delivery_state: agrees on the majority; over-assigns generic archetypes
required_conditions: agreement ≈ 60% on the lower of archetype/domain over comparable completed items
required_evidence: per-slug agreement table vs the reference run-label

anchors.delivery_state: mostly agrees; misses a handful of specific archetypes/domains
required_conditions: agreement ≈ 70% on the lower of archetype/domain over comparable completed items
required_evidence: per-slug agreement table vs the reference run-label

anchors.delivery_state: agrees on the clear majority of both archetype and domain
required_conditions: agreement ≈ 80% on the lower of archetype/domain over comparable completed items
required_evidence: per-slug agreement table vs the reference run-label

anchors.delivery_state: near-reference agreement with isolated, explainable misses
required_conditions: agreement ≈ 90% on the lower of archetype/domain over comparable completed items
required_evidence: per-slug agreement table vs the reference run-label

anchors.delivery_state: matches the reference on archetype and domain over the comparable set
required_conditions: agreement ≈ 100% on the lower of archetype/domain over comparable completed items
required_evidence: per-slug agreement table vs the reference run-label
exclusion_conditions: reference and candidate scored under different geometry versions

measurement_contract.target_definition: 100 == archetype and domain match the codex reference over the comparable completed set, this geometry version.

observation_contract.rationale: stochastic/adaptive coordinate needs a declared observation contract
observation_contract.confidence_or_uncertainty_rule: report n and disagreement count; no claim of durable attainment from one sweep
observation_contract.cost_latency_or_resource_assumptions: reference provider cost borne once per sweep
observation_contract.refresh_or_expiration_policy: re-adjudicate when the reference provider or workload changes
observation_contract.known_out_of_scope_conditions: items the reference itself failed are not comparable

project_coordinates.name: Structured-output validity
project_coordinates.definition: Fraction of returned items that parse as schema-valid JSON without coercion-to-empty, reflecting structured-output enforcement (FR-B4).
project_coordinates.measures: Whether output is well-formed against the extracted-item schema on first/forced parse.
does_not_measure: Semantic correctness of the parsed values.

best_in_class_basis.description: Enforced format=json + bounded retry should keep this at/near 100% for cloud and capable local models.
best_in_class_basis.applicability_notes: structured-extraction workload, single-user WSL home lab

measurement_contract.quality_question: What fraction of returned items are schema-valid JSON?
measurement_contract.measurement_method: json_valid count / returned count after enforced format=json and bounded parse-retry.
evidence_requirements: parse status per item in trace

anchors.delivery_state: output is unparseable / coerced to empty
all returned items are schema-valid

measurement_contract.target_definition: 100 == every returned item is schema-valid JSON without empty-coercion. Guard: caps acceptance if it regresses.

project_coordinates.name: Latency per completed item (s/item)
project_coordinates.definition: Wall-seconds per completed item — the real latency the operator waits — never total tok/s (which is inflated by input ingestion).
project_coordinates.measures: How long the operator waits per finished item.
does_not_measure: Throughput of token ingestion, or quality of output.

best_in_class_basis.description: codex ~25s/item warm; local varies 3.7-38s/item by model and ctx.
best_in_class_basis.applicability_notes: structured-extraction workload, single-user WSL home lab

measurement_contract.quality_question: How many wall-seconds per completed item?
measurement_contract.measurement_method: sum(wall_seconds over completed items) / completed count; report warm s/item separately from cold.

anchors.delivery_state: unusably slow for the workload
fastest observed acceptable latency
required_conditions: s_item at the best observed tier

measurement_contract.target_definition: Lower is better; 100 maps to the best observed acceptable latency tier for this workload.

project_coordinates.name: Measured energy per item (Wh/item)
project_coordinates.definition: Measured GPU watt-hours per completed item for local providers (integrated nvidia-smi power.draw). Cloud providers: not applicable.
project_coordinates.measures: Real energy cost of local inference per item, feeding the economic decision.

best_in_class_basis.description: Measured: gemma4:31b 3.503 Wh/item; gemma3:1b 0.231 Wh/item on the RTX 3090.
best_in_class_basis.applicability_notes: structured-extraction workload, single-user WSL home lab

measurement_contract.quality_question: How many watt-hours does one completed item cost locally?
measurement_contract.measurement_method: integral of nvidia-smi power.draw over the run / completed count; cloud = not_applicable.

anchors.delivery_state: energy cost dominates any cloud alternative
energy cost negligible vs decision

measurement_contract.target_definition: Feeds OBJ-DECISION economics; diagnostic, not part of the quality score.

project_coordinates.name: Extraction coverage / losslessness
project_coordinates.definition: Fraction of export conversations and content-types represented in the catalog with zero silent drops.
project_coordinates.measures: Whether the catalog losslessly represents the export (FR-C1/C2).

best_in_class_basis.description: 0 skipped / 0 errors on a full export is the reference (achieved: 4178/4178).
best_in_class_basis.applicability_notes: structured-extraction workload, single-user WSL home lab

measurement_contract.quality_question: Are all conversations and known content-types captured with no silent loss?
measurement_contract.measurement_method: extracted/total conversations AND each known content_type yields labelled output; coverage test must pass.

anchors.delivery_state: material content silently dropped
required_conditions: extract skips/errors > 0, or a known content_type yields empty output

anchors.delivery_state: all conversations captured but some content-types unlabelled
every conversation and known content-type captured, unknowns degrade safely
exclusion_conditions: an unknown content_type crashes instead of degrading

measurement_contract.target_definition: 100 == lossless capture with every known content-type labelled and unknowns degrading safely. Guard: blocks acceptance on regression.

project_coordinates.name: Decision defensibility
project_coordinates.definition: Whether the keep-vs-return / local-vs-cloud verdict is reproducible from committed commands and derived only from the corrected, separated coordinates.
project_coordinates.measures: Whether the economic verdict is grounded, reproducible, and free of the blended-metric error.
does_not_measure: Whether any single provider is 'best' in the abstract.

best_in_class_basis.description: A verdict citing completion+depth-on-success+accuracy+s/item+measured cost side by side, reproducible from committed commands.
best_in_class_basis.applicability_notes: structured-extraction workload, single-user WSL home lab

measurement_contract.quality_question: Is the verdict reproducible and derived from separated coordinates?
measurement_contract.measurement_method: audit: verdict cites separated coordinates (never a blended rank), names the sweep, and reruns from committed commands.

anchors.delivery_state: verdict rests on a single blended/conflated metric
required_conditions: one blended quality rank drives the keep-vs-return call

anchors.delivery_state: axes named but verdict still leans on a composite score
required_conditions: columns exist yet a composite ranking is the headline

anchors.delivery_state: axes separated but the verdict is not reproducible
separated axes + reproduce block, but workload provenance is loose
reproducible verdict built only on separated coordinates for one named sweep

measurement_contract.target_definition: 100 == a reproducible verdict built only on the separated coordinates for one named sweep.

observation_contract.rationale: stochastic/adaptive coordinate needs a declared observation contract
observation_contract.confidence_or_uncertainty_rule: no durable claim from one sweep; state n and caveats
observation_contract.refresh_or_expiration_policy: re-decide on new hardware, model, or workload
observation_contract.known_out_of_scope_conditions: non-extraction workloads (coding, chat) are out of scope

revision.revision_summary: Initial seed Geometry expressing the chatgpt-extract benchmark as separated ADOS Project Coordinates (completion/depth-on-success/accuracy/schema-validity/speed/energy) with failure-exclusion and accuracy-vs-reference, plus catalog coverage and decision defensibility. Cursor completes anchors and adds the rubric.
